title: kengyilia alatavica var. longiglumis
intro: Kengyilia alatavica var. longiglumis is a type of grass found in the mountainous regions of Central Asia, belonging to the family Poaceae. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Kengyilia alatavica var. longiglumis include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and improve overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as an infusion or decoction to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and safety.
